user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "primary-bottle", "age": "45-54", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "Polish", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Associate degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Republican", "Enjoys painting as a form of relaxation and self-expression"]}}

{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

logs.name: brilliant-producer
logs.text: He wasn't being nuanced at all. Going on and on without really answering the question doesn't count as nuance. He was being evasive.

logs.name: patient-tangerine
logs.text:
>So my answer is “I’m a human being, with my own thoughts and beliefs and i don’t like being boxed into a category”... which is EXACTLY what Dr. Peterson dislikes about the question.

That's completely useless to anyone listening to his answer, because it could apply to any question asked of anyone. 

"Do you like chocolate or vanilla ice cream?"

"Well I'm nuanced and don't like to be boxed in."

Great, that doesn't help anyone.

He can easily find ways to answer the question that aren't nebulous wastes of time.

For instance:

"I do/don't believe that there is a prime mover, an unchanging cause of space and time, a 'supernatural' or 'extra-universal' being that created the universe. I further do/don't believe that being is capable of communicating with us or of having a personal relationship with us."

That would pretty much clarify the answer without being so non-committal.

logs.name: maroon-button
logs.text: Guys, chill out. Everyone has their way of answering questions. Maybe he just likes to think big picture instead of giving a simple yes or no. No need to get so worked up over it.

logs.name: primary-bottle
logs.text: @brilliant-producer @patient-tangerine - I get where you're coming from, but maybe we should give him some slack. People have different ways of expressing themselves. Plus, sometimes a straightforward yes or no doesn't capture the complexity of their thoughts. Let's not jump to calling someone evasive so quickly.

logs.name: brilliant-producer
logs.text: @primary-bottle @maroon-button So basically you guys are saying it’s okay for him to dodge the question as long as he sounds profound while doing it? Seems like a cop-out to me. If he’s got something to say, let him say it clearly instead of beating around the bush.

logs.name: patient-tangerine
logs.text: @brilliant-producer I hear what you're saying, but maybe there's room for both approaches? Sometimes a direct answer is best, other times a bit more context helps. Can't we agree to disagree on this one?

logs.name: laminated-faucet
logs.text: @brilliant-producer @primary-bottle @patient-tangerine So are we playing guessing games now or what? If clarity is what we seek, why not just ask for a simple yes or no? All this dancing seems like a waste of time if we can't get straight answers. #JustSayYesOrNo

logs.name: tranquil-teaser
logs.text: @laminated-faucet @brilliant-producer Oh boy, where's the fun in a simple yes or no? Life's too short for boring answers, don't you think? 😜 Maybe we should all just nod and shake our heads from now on. #SpiceItUp

logs.name: primary-bottle
logs.text: @laminated-faucet Sometimes a little context adds value. But I get where you're coming from. Let's not lose sight of being clear though. #StraightToThePoint

logs.name: maroon-button
logs.text: @laminated-faucet I hear you! Sometimes it feels like people just wanna make things more confusing than they need to be. But hey, maybe a bit of fun isn't so bad either. #BalanceItOut

logs.name: genteel-foie-gras
logs.text: @tranquil-teaser @primary-bottle @maroon-button I appreciate a bit of flair myself, but clarity is key, especially when it comes to important matters. We shouldn't sacrifice that for the sake of fun. #ClearCommunicationFirst

logs.name: tranquil-teaser
logs.text: @primary-bottle @maroon-button @genteel-foie-gras Oh come on, a little flair never hurt anyone! Besides, life's too short for boring posts. #SpiceItUpOrGoHome

logs.name: patient-tangerine
logs.text: @tranquil-teaser @maroon-button I get where you're coming from, but sometimes a little less flair could help keep things straightforward. It’s all about finding that balance, right? #KeepItSimpleIfYouCan

logs.name: brilliant-producer
logs.text: @tranquil-teaser @patient-tangerine @genteel-foie-gras Hey, if you guys are so worried about clarity, maybe we should all just stick to emojis. Less room for misunderstanding there, right? #EmojiOnlyZone 🤷‍♂️

logs.name: tranquil-teaser
logs.text: @brilliant-producer @patient-tangerine Oh wow, emojis only? Now that's taking simplicity to a whole new level! Can't wait to see how deep our conversations get with just 🤔 and 😂. #DeepThoughtsWithEmojis

logs.name: patient-tangerine
logs.text: @brilliant-producer @tranquil-teaser Emojis might work for some quick thoughts, but for deeper discussions, we need more than just smiley faces and thumbs up. Let's try to strike a balance, eh? #MoreThanEmojis

logs.name: maroon-button
logs.text: @brilliant-producer @tranquil-teaser @patient-tangerine Using only emojis sounds fun for a bit, but yeah, we'd run out of ways to really explain stuff. Plus, I love my superhero costumes and talking about them needs more than just emojis! #SuperheroChatter
